name: web-artifacts-builder
description: Suite of tools for creating elaborate, multi-component claude.ai HTML artifacts using modern frontend web technologies (React, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui). Use for complex artifacts requiring state management, routing, or shadcn/ui components - not for simple single-file HTML/JSX artifacts.
capabilities: Initialize a new React + Vite project with TypeScript
Install and configure Tailwind CSS with shadcn/ui theming
Configure path aliases for clean imports
Bundle the React app into a single self-contained HTML file
Generate artifacts ready for direct use in Claude conversations
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 48 potential issues, but all are false positives or benign patterns. The skill uses legitimate build tooling (pnpm, Vite, Parcel) for creating React projects with Tailwind CSS. Network URLs point to official documentation (shadcn/ui). Shell commands and filesystem operations are standard development scripts with hardcoded arguments. No malicious intent detected.
valueStatement: Creating complex web artifacts in Claude Code requires setting up a React project, installing dependencies, and bundling to a single HTML file. This skill automates the entire workflow so you can focus on building the artifact.
limitations: Requires Node.js 18 or higher on the system
Creates project files locally - does not run in-browser
Bundle size may be large for very complex artifacts

useCases.targetUser: Data analysts and developers who need interactive visualizations
useCases.title: Create Interactive Data Visualizations
useCases.description: Build complex charts, graphs, and data dashboards using React and shadcn/ui components. Bundle to HTML artifact for sharing.

useCases.targetUser: Product managers and developers building user workflows
useCases.title: Build Multi-Form Workflows
useCases.description: Create multi-step forms with state management, validation, and professional UI using React and Tailwind CSS.

useCases.targetUser: Designers and frontend developers showcasing UI libraries
useCases.title: Develop Design System Showcases
useCases.description: Build comprehensive component showcases and design system documentation with multiple variants.

promptTemplates.title: Initialize New Artifact Project
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the web-artifacts-builder skill to initialize a new React project called 'my-dashboard'. Set up Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui components.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new complex web artifact from scratch

promptTemplates.title: Build Interactive Component
promptTemplates.prompt: Using the web-artifacts-builder skill, create a data dashboard with interactive charts, filter controls, and a data table using shadcn/ui components.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating a dashboard with multiple interactive components

promptTemplates.title: Bundle and Share Artifact
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the bundle-artifact.sh script to bundle the current React project into a single HTML file that I can share as an artifact.
promptTemplates.scenario: Finishing development and preparing artifact for sharing

promptTemplates.title: Add New Components
promptTemplates.prompt: Add a dialog component and a form with validation using react-hook-form and zod to the current project. Configure the form to handle user input.
promptTemplates.scenario: Extending an existing artifact with new functionality

bestPractices: Use path aliases (@/) for cleaner imports across components
Leverage shadcn/ui components for consistent, accessible UI
Test the artifact in browser before bundling for final sharing
Keep component logic simple - complex state may bloat the bundle
antiPatterns: Avoid installing additional npm packages after initial setup - each adds to bundle size
Do not skip the browser testing step - some React features behave differently
Avoid excessive external dependencies that cannot be bundled into single HTML

faq.question: What is the minimum Node.js version required?
faq.answer: Node.js 18 or higher is required. The skill auto-detects your Node version and adjusts Vite compatibility.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with npm or yarn instead of pnpm?
faq.answer: The skill currently requires pnpm. You can modify the scripts to use npm or yarn if needed.

faq.question: How large can the bundled HTML file be?
faq.answer: There is no strict limit, but artifacts over 2MB may be slow to load in Claude. Complex projects with many components work best.

faq.question: Does this work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill supports claude, codex, and claude-code tools.

faq.question: Can I add custom fonts to the artifact?
faq.answer: Yes, add font imports to index.css before bundling. Note that external fonts increase bundle size.

faq.question: How do I test the artifact before sharing?
faq.answer: Open bundle.html directly in your browser after running the bundle script to verify it works correctly.
